name: Slint
description: Declarative DSL, GPL/commercial dual-license, corporate-backed (SixtyFPS GmbH), cross-platform including embedded

license.comment: Triple-licensed: GPL-3.0 for open source, royalty-free proprietary license for desktop/mobile/web, and paid commercial license. The crates.io manifest declares only GPL-3.0-only, which is tagged here as the primary open-source license.

first-release.comment: Project started in spring 2020 under the name SixtyFPS; renamed to Slint in 2022. First `slint` crate publish on crates.io was February 1, 2022; earlier `sixtyfps` crate releases go back to 2020.

maintenance-status.comment: 1.16 shipped April 16, 2026, 1.16.1 on April 23, 2026; master branch averages hundreds of commits per month from SixtyFPS GmbH staff and community contributors.

maturity.comment: 1.0 released April 2023; now at 1.16 with a stable API, commercial customers, and a production track record on desktop and embedded.

corporate-backing.value: SixtyFPS GmbH — remote team distributed across Germany, Finland, and the US; funds development via a dual open-source/commercial license model.

implementation.comment: Core is pure Rust. Optional Qt backend links against system Qt for native styling, but the default feature set ships femtovg/Skia/software renderers with no C/C++ toolkit dependency.

rendering-mode.comment: Retained scene tree authored in the .slint DSL; property bindings update the tree when reactive dependencies change.

reactivity-model.comment: Fine-grained reactive property bindings (signal-like) in the DSL drive the retained tree; Rust/C++ side wires up callbacks for events and invokes setters on generated property accessors.

binding-target.comment: Slint renders its own widgets; an optional Qt backend exists for native styling but is not the default target.

thread-model.comment: Event loop must run on the main thread; cross-thread communication goes through slint::invoke_from_event_loop or slint::spawn_local.

renderer.comment: Default femtovg renderer uses OpenGL ES 2.0; Skia and pure-software renderers are opt-in. Qt backend renders with the system's native toolkit. Slint 1.12 added FemtoVGWGPURenderer so apps can share a wgpu device with crates like Bevy.

gpu-acceleration.comment: Default femtovg and Skia renderers are GPU-accelerated; software renderer exists for MCUs and headless targets.

vector-rendering.comment: Path element renders resolution-independent SVG-style primitives; @image-url can load SVGs.

custom-shaders.comment: Slint 1.12 added WGPU integration enabling apps to share a wgpu device and run custom render passes (e.g. embedding Bevy). Older OpenGL underlay/texture APIs also allow custom GL rendering.

embedded-custom-rendering.comment: Dedicated examples (opengl_underlay, opengl_texture, wgpu_texture, Bevy integration) embed custom 3D/2D content inside the Slint UI.

windows.comment: Windows 10 and 11 on x86-64 and Windows 11 on aarch64 are officially supported.

macos.comment: macOS 14 Sonoma, 15 Sequoia, and macOS 26 on Apple Silicon are officially supported.

linux-wayland.comment: Docs state Slint runs on Linux distributions using Wayland or X-Windows; Wayland is a first-class target via winit.

ios.comment: iOS introduced as a tech preview in Slint 1.12 (June 2025); cross-compilation, Xcode integration, and App Store deployment are documented but support is still labeled preview as of 1.16, so the boolean is held to `false` per RESEARCH.md's stricter reading.

android.comment: Android support stabilized in Slint 1.5 (2024) via the backend-android-activity-05 feature.

web-wasm.comment: Rust apps cross-compile to WebAssembly via the Winit+FemtoVG backend and run in the browser. Slint's own docs state WASM is geared toward demos and is not recommended for general-purpose web apps (no screen-reader support, custom text rendering, non-standard HTML semantics).

embedded-no-std.comment: First-class support for microcontrollers: Raspberry Pi, STM32, RP2040, ESP, Zephyr; software renderer supports no_std-capable targets.

authoring-style.comment: UI is authored in .slint files (own DSL) and compiled to native Rust/C++/JS/Python code; host-language bindings then wire up data and callbacks.

hot-reload.comment: slint-viewer auto-reloads .slint files; the VS Code extension / slint-lsp provide an in-editor live preview that updates on save without recompiling the Rust/C++ host.

layout-system.comment: HorizontalLayout / VerticalLayout behave as a simplified flexbox; GridLayout is a grid. Slint 1.16 adds a more general experimental FlexboxLayout.

styling-approach.comment: .slint files are the stylesheet DSL; global Palette and selectable widget styles (Fluent, Material, Cupertino, Cosmic) form a theme API; inline property overrides per widget are pervasive.

theme-switching.comment: Palette supports runtime light/dark switching that responds to system color-scheme changes.

accessibility.comment: Winit backend integrates with AccessKit for cross-platform screen-reader support (enabled by default via the `accessibility` feature). Coverage is growing — text-input exposure and large-listview performance remain open issues.

internationalization.comment: Built-in @tr macro with gettext (.mo) runtime loading, bundled .po files for WASM/MCU targets, plural forms, contextual translations, and slint-tr-extractor tooling. RTL is not explicitly documented.

widget-breadth.comment: std-widgets covers Button, CheckBox, Switch, Slider, SpinBox, LineEdit, TextEdit, ComboBox, TabWidget, ScrollView, GroupBox, ProgressIndicator, ListView, StandardListView, StandardTableView, Dialog, StandardButton; 1.x added MenuBar/ContextMenuArea and (in 1.16) StyledText with markdown. Missing: tree view, first-party charts.

data-grid-table.comment: StandardTableView widget is provided; backed by ListView virtualization.

rich-text-markdown.comment: StyledText element and @markdown(...) macro landed in 1.16 as a first step toward full rich-text rendering.

tree-outline.comment: No first-party TreeView widget; the documented pattern is flattening a tree into a ListView model.

native-menus.comment: MenuBar / ContextMenuArea / Menu / MenuItem / MenuSeparator elements. Slint uses the `muda` crate on Windows and macOS for native rendering; on macOS the menu bar is hoisted to the system bar.

file-dialogs.comment: Slint has a Dialog container widget but no first-party native file-open/save dialog; apps integrate the external `rfd` crate.

charts-plotting.comment: No built-in chart widget. The `plotter` example demonstrates custom chart rendering using the Path element.

custom-widgets.comment: Authoring custom widgets in the .slint DSL is straightforward: declare a component with properties, callbacks, states, and animations — little boilerplate and full hot reload.

async-runtime-compatible.comment: Slint provides its own event loop with slint::spawn_local; tokio futures can be awaited by spawning the tokio runtime on another thread and using invoke_from_event_loop to marshal results back. async-std/smol aren't called out as first-class, though the pattern is the same.

system-tray.comment: Not built-in. The desktop-ready roadmap post discusses adding tray icons and context menus via dbus/macOS/Windows APIs; users today rely on external crates like tray-icon.

notifications.comment: No built-in OS notification API; users integrate external crates (e.g. notify-rust).

clipboard.comment: TextInput/LineEdit/TextEdit expose copy/cut/paste functions and keyboard shortcuts. A general-purpose programmatic clipboard API (outside text widgets) is still tracked as a feature request.

drag-and-drop.comment: 1.16 introduces in-app drag-and-drop but only between elements within a single window, and the feature is gated behind SLINT_ENABLE_EXPERIMENTAL_FEATURES=1. OS-level / cross-window drag-and-drop is on the roadmap and requires upstream winit work.

embedded-webview.comment: No first-party webview element. Integrating wry/webview would be user-managed through a custom OpenGL/WGPU underlay.

binary-size.comment: Not independently measured here. Typical stripped release builds of a minimal Slint app with the default femtovg renderer land in the 10-30 MB range; software-only renderer can be much smaller on MCU targets.

startup-time.comment: Not independently measured. Slint emphasizes low overhead and targets MCUs; desktop cold starts are generally in the 50-200 ms range. Verify locally for your build.

memory-footprint.comment: Not independently measured. Idle memory of a minimal desktop app typically sits in the 20-80 MB band; MCU targets drop far below 20 MB by design.

idle-redraw.comment: Retained-mode renderer redraws on property change; animations opt into continuous redraw while running.

dependency-count.comment: Default feature set pulls in winit, femtovg, image, cosmic-text, etc.; typical cargo tree is in the 50-200 range. Not independently measured with cargo tree.

documentation-quality.comment: Full docs site with language reference, widget reference, platform guides, tutorials, and per-language quickstarts for Rust/C++/Node/Python. Blog regularly covers new features and migration notes.

api-ergonomics.comment: Rust side is builder-ish with generated typed accessors and ModelRc for data; .slint DSL handles the view layer. Minor friction from the two-language split (e.g. generated code model) but overall idiomatic.

examples-count.comment: 40+ official examples across desktop, mobile, embedded/MCU, WebAssembly, 3D/WGPU integrations, and games.

interactive-playground.comment: SlintPad is the official browser-based editor/playground for .slint code.

ide-tooling.comment: slint-lsp language server with autocomplete + live preview; official Visual Studio Code, Kate, and Neovim integrations; slint-viewer acts as a runtime inspector/previewer with auto-reload.

dev-tools-inspector.comment: Live-preview and slint-viewer double as widget-tree inspectors; the VS Code extension surfaces a design-mode inspector.

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time downloads of the top-level `slint` crate as of 2026-04-24 (~180k/month recent). Does not include the earlier `sixtyfps` crate.

notable-production-users.value: OTIV (autonomous rail vehicles), SK Signet (EV chargers), WesAudio (DAW plugins), Nexter Robotics, Embex GmbH, Jasic Technology Europe, Toradex, and various embedded/MCU deployments.

community-size.comment: ~22k GitHub stars, active Chat/Mattermost and GitHub Discussions, regular external contributions, plus a paid SixtyFPS GmbH core team.

ecosystem-crates.comment: Official Rust/C++/Node/Python bindings, Figma-to-Slint plugin, community widget libraries (SurrealismUI, vivi, coop). Third-party widget/theme ecosystem is smaller than older toolkits but actively expanding.
